Description: Tests whether the business has a formally governed source system for leads, opportunities, customers, invoices, renewals, and management KPIs, rather than conflicting CRM exports, finance files, and shadow spreadsheets.
Tests whether users capture required ownership, stage, date, amount, status, and next-step data at the point where work happens, instead of correcting records only when reporting is due.

Tests whether account, contact, deal, invoice, and service records are complete, current, deduplicated, and categorised consistently enough to support funnel, forecast, billing, cash, and retention reporting.
Tests whether the data shows the full lifecycle from lead through qualification, proposal, close, invoicing, payment, onboarding, and renewal, including handoffs, aging, backlog, and drop-off.
Tests whether core management KPIs have approved formulas, inclusion rules, targets, owners, and refresh logic so the same metric means the same thing across dashboards and reports.
Tests whether dashboards and management packs are built from trusted data, focused on decisions and exceptions, and consistent across leadership, managers, and operating teams.
Tests whether named owners, SOPs, review cadence, change control, and issue-resolution routines exist to keep data, KPI logic, and reporting accurate over time.
